steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence ("temperature reductions were observed in the air immediately surrounding the mushrooms") within the passage. The sentence immediately after it says those "slight" reductions inspired an air-cooling device — so the underlined sentence is the finding that leads to a practical application.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'empirical evidence to bolster the claim that certain fungal species maintain a hypothermic state': the claim earlier is about evaporative cooling in the fungi themselves, not in the surrounding air; the underlined sentence extends the finding, not bolsters that prior claim. ✗
  • Choice B — 'an unexpected observation that motivated the study': the study came first; this observation is a result of the study, not what started it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'a finding central to the text's discussion of a practical application': the air-temperature reduction directly inspired the cooling device described next — the underlined sentence is the pivot from discovery to application. ✓
  • Choice D — 'a tangential finding the later experiment was designed to explain': the later experiment was the device built from the finding, not designed to explain it; and the finding is central, not tangential. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
